title: hieracium prenanthoides subsp. gredense
intro: Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. gredense, also known as Gred's Hawkweed, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Iberian Peninsula. It belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is a type of flowering plant with medicinal properties, primarily used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. gredense can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, and its dried leaves and roots can be used in herbal remedies.
